findings.title: AuthService bypasses repository abstraction
findings.runtime_scope_justification: src/auth/service.py is application code running at production runtime, accessed only by internal services.
findings.failure_mode_justification: Bypassing the repository means queries skip caching and audit logging, producing stale or untracked data access.
findings.evidence_quality_justification: Lines 42-58 show a direct DB query via sqlalchemy session; AuthRepository.get_user() at repo.py:10 provides the intended abstraction.
findings.trace_origin_justification: Traced within auth module: AuthService.authenticate() calls session.query() directly instead of AuthRepository.get_user().
findings.effort_to_fix_justification: Inject AuthRepository into AuthService and replace the direct query with repo.get_user().
findings.issue: AuthService reads the database directly instead of going through AuthRepository.
findings.why_it_matters: All other services route DB access through repositories per the project's stated architecture (CLAUDE.md).
findings.suggested_fix: Inject AuthRepository into AuthService and replace the direct query with repo.get_user().
